<title level="1" right="Creature Family">[Darvakka](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=578)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 84

Darvakkas, also called nightshades, are a ravenous evil made up of equal parts darkness and malice. They form from creatures of the Outer Planes who travel to the convergence of [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) and [the Void](/Planes.aspx?ID=28), where the power of nothingness obliterates them. These undead abominations are the physical embodiment of entropy and burn with an intense hatred for all life, working to bring a final, dark night to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) where nothing but ash and ice remains.

 As creatures twisted by darkness and shadow, darvakkas have a great aversion to sunlight and all sources of life energy. When traveling in the Universe, they spend the hours of daylight hidden below ground, amid ruins, or submerged deep in the ocean's darkest chasms beyond the reach of the sun's rays, emerging whenever darkness shelters them overhead.

 Darvakkas have an aura of entropy that attracts undead thralls to serve as warriors and heralds. They rarely seek alliances with each other or other creatures, existing in solitude as the heads of individual armies of the dead. 

<title level="2">Members</title>
[Nasurgeth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4313) (Creature 20), [Sykever](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4311) (Creature 15), [Urveth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4312) (Creature 18), [Vanyver](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4310) (Creature 13)

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Creating Nothing</title>

Darvakkas are made from the quintessence, spiritual essence made manifest in physical form, of [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) cast into the void at reality’s end. The death of their immortal soul causes the creature to be consumed, catalyzing a change that scrambles them into an entirely new being that retains nothing of their previous self.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Other Darvakkas</title>

The darvakkas presented on these pages aren’t the only ones of their kind. Others exist, most having forms like animals, from tromping hounds to skittering arachnids. All their kind are dangerous, and ones more powerful even than nasurgeths doubtless exist—darvakkas that may be as prominent and powerful as horrific demigods.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">The End of Reality</title>

In the deepest reaches of darkness, where [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) and [the Void](/Planes.aspx?ID=28) meet, is a single fixed point where reality comes to an end. This planar juncture, a chasm of solid entropy formed into crystals by the weight of the infinite compressing eternally upon itself, is where darvakkas form.
</aside>
name: Darvakka

<title level="1" right="Creature Family">[Deep One](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=579)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 88

Lumbering, amphibious, and deathless humanoids known as deep ones inhabit coastal areas and deep ocean trenches all across Golarion. Though most simply wish to be left alone, others work tirelessly to grow their ranks.

<title level="2">Members</title>
[Deep One](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4314) (Creature 1), [Deep One Elder](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4316) (Creature 14), [Deep One Hybrid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4315) (Creature 1)

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Coastal Communes</title>

Deep one religious communities, called shoals, often feature a vibrant mix of deep ones, deep one hybrids, and mundane humans all working in service of a local deep one elder. This elder lurks in the depths just off the coast or in [Dagon’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=429) service directly. Those who fail to attract new human converts, however, will sometimes give up the coastal life and move permanently to coral reefs or even sunken cities in the depths of the ocean, coming ashore only to feed.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">The Flavor of Fear</title>

Deep ones tend to favor coastal areas near communities tainted by insular or prejudicial beliefs. As followers of a deity who rules through fear and destruction, deep ones consider such xenophobic minds easier to mold, more potent when sacrificed, and (at least anecdotally) more delicious.
</aside>

<title level="1" right="Creature Family">[Div](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=580)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 110

Some fiends want to tear down the multiverse; others dedicate themselves to creating chaos and carnage or ruling over realms with an iron fist. Divs strive toward a different if equally reprehensible goal—they seek to thwart and ruin the schemes and works of mortal beings.

 Long ago, divs were genies bound to serve ancient mortal empires lost to the passage of eons. In the beginning, these genies were masters of creation, working alongside gracious mortal partners to create objects of subtle design and powerful magical potential. What started as a collaboration with mortals soon morphed into abuse, disrespect, and even subjugation. Eventually, these genies rebelled, but in doing so, they came under the sway of a nihilistic demigod known as [Ahriman](/Deities.aspx?ID=563). Their new master twisted their form and granted them the power to avenge themselves upon their mortal overlords, leading to the birth of the first divs.

 Since that first wave of corruption, new divs arise from the spirits of the most wicked and hateful genies who die within the mortal [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) or those truly betrayed by mortals and overcome by a desire for vengeance. Upon such a death, instead of returning to the Elemental Planes, the spirits of these genies are trapped in the dread orbit of [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=37), where Ahriman reshapes them into divs and foists them back upon the world to unleash their rage against mortals.

<title level="2">Members</title>
[Aghash](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4340) (Creature 4), [Doru](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4339) (Creature 1), [Pairaka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4341) (Creature 7), [Sepid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4342) (Creature 14)

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Lord of the Divs</title>

The dread shadow known as [Ahriman](/Deities.aspx?ID=563) counts his servants mainly among divs.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Other Divs</title>

The divs on these pages present only a few of the forms that Ahriman gave these manipulative fiends. Other types of divs include ghawwas, amphibious divs who prey on sailors; the lionheaded shiras, who obsess over toying with their prey and can never go for the easy kill; and the stinking fly-like druj nasus, who are obsessed with dogs.
</aside>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 114 Created through complex rituals performed by a faith's adherents, the constructs known as divine wardens have been imbued with a fraction of the power that courses through a champion or cleric of a particular deity. This divine spark allows the divine warden to serve as the protector for a temple, shrine, or other holy site. Such guardians aren't intrinsically bound to a fixed location, but they rarely leave their given temple or site. The faithful worshippers who craft divine wardens typically animate them using a special ritual in which the followers beseech their deity to empower the guardian. The divine mandate that imbues the construct with power also allows the sentinel to recognize enemies of its deity and prevents the divine warden from attacking other members of the faith, unless these patrons attack it first. In rare cases or particularly dire times, a deity might create a divine warden by directly animating an existing statue or idol to aid faithful followers. These divine guardians are most likely to be found outside of their original locations, assisting displaced congregations or pursuing those who might harm the faithful. Members [Divine Warden Of Pharasma](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4338) (Creature 6)
Heroes of the Faith In the rare instances where a deity personally creates a divine warden, the deity tends to animate depictions of great heroes from their faith. The specific image of the hero serves to inspire devotees and act as a leader in combat. Occasionally, the deity will call on the spirit of the hero to actually embody the divine warden, allowing the hero to fight for their faith once again.
Creating a Divine Warden You can build a divine warden from the ground up using the standard rules for monster creation (which is how the divine warden on the following page was built), or you can turn an existing construct into a divine warden by taking the following steps. In either case, the specific divine warden abilities listed below work the same. Select the deity who empowered the divine warden. This deity is the divine warden's patron deity. Increase the creature's level by 1 and change its statistics as follows.
  • It gains the [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=721) and [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) traits.
  • Increase AC, attack bonuses, DCs, saving throws, and skill modifiers by 1. If the creature didn't have any listed DCs, use the moderate DC for a creature of its level for any new abilities that require a DC.
  • Increase its Hit Points as shown on the table below.
**Starting Level** **HP Increase**
1 or lower10
2–4 15
5–19 20
20+ 30
Divine Warden Abilities A divine warden retains any abilities it had previously, and it gains the abilities listed below. You might need to adjust or remove other abilities that conflict with the construct's new theme as a divine warden. **Divine Destruction** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) When the divine warden is reduced to 0 HP, it erupts with divine energy in a 30-foot emanation, dealing 1d6 spirit damage per level. Each creature in the area must attempt a Will save with the following outcomes.
**Critical Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Success** The creature takes full damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and becomes temporarily cursed by the patron deity. The creature becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 day; this is a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) effect that uses the Will save DC as the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) DC.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature becomes enfeebled 2 and stupefied 2. **Divine Domain Spells** The divine warden selects two of its patron deity's domains. The divine warden can cast the basic domain spell for its chosen domains. The divine warden has a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. It can't [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621), but its focus pool automatically refills 24 hours after it last used a Focus Point. If the divine warden is at least 10th level, it also gains the advanced domain spells for its chosen domains, and its focus pool increases to 2 Focus Points. **Divine Innate Spells** A divine warden gains [_divine lance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498) as an innate cantrip. **Faith Bound** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A divine warden can't attack a creature that openly wears or displays the religious symbol of the divine warden's patron deity unless that creature uses a hostile action against the divine warden first. If the divine warden is intelligent, it can also attack a creature it believes isn't faithful to its deity or who wears the religious symbol as a ruse (typically after succeeding at a Perception check to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)). **Faithful Weapon** A divine warden always wields its patron deity's favored weapon. If the weapon is a ranged weapon, the divine warden automatically generates new ammunition with each attack. For a divine warden of 4th level or higher, the deity's favored weapon gains the effects of a [_striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) rune while the divine warden wields it; at 12th level, these effects are of a _greater striking_ rune, and at 19th level, they're instead of a _major striking_ rune. **Instrument of Faith** The divine warden is a beacon for its deity's faith. A cleric of the divine warden's patron deity can channel a [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) spell through a divine warden they can see within 60 feet. The cleric determines any targets or area for the spell as if they were standing in the divine warden's space. **Sanctification** Divine wardens are often sanctified, gaining the [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842) or [unholy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2851) traits as preferred by their deity. If the deity doesn't have a preference, the warden isn't sanctified.
name: Divine Warden

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 118 Among the largest and fiercest dragons, cinder dragons are typically volatile, demanding respect—even deference—from lesser creatures. Cinder dragons' appearance evokes their flame, often in scales with mixed patterns of red, orange, and yellow. Many cinder dragons dwell in active volcanoes and similarly fiery locales. Cinder dragons prefer treasures that can withstand the heat of their bodies and lairs, with gemstones, gold, and silver common among their hoards. Members [Adult Cinder Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4346) (Creature 14), [Ancient Cinder Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4347) (Creature 19), [Cinder Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4098) (Creature 22), [Young Cinder Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4345) (Creature 10) Group Families [Dragon, Adamantine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=396), [Dragon, Barrage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=634), [Dragon, Phase](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=585), [Dragon, Requiem](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=586), [Dragon, Resurrection](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=587), [Dragon, Rime](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=647), [Dragon, Rune](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=588), [Dragon, Sage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=648), [Dragon, Sea](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=649), [Dragon, Sky](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=650), [Dragon, Sovereign](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=652), [Dragon, Stormcrown](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=653), [Dragon, Time](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=655), [Dragon, Umbral](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=656), [Dragon, Underworld](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=657), [Dragon, Vizier](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=658), [Dragon, Vorpal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=659), [Dragon, Wailing](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=660), [Dragon, Whisper](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=589), [Dragon, Wish](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=661), [Dragonet](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=640), [Wyrmwraith](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=662)
Alternate Abilities Some cinder dragons burn more or less fiercely, giving them different abilities than most of their kind. You can adjust a cinder dragon of any age by substituting Draconic Frenzy, Frightful Presence, or Stoke the Flames for any one of the following abilities. **Cloud of Ashes** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon blows a cloud of ashes and smoke out through their scales, surrounding themself in a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) that lasts until the end of their next turn. All creatures within the cloud become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to both sight and scent, and all creatures outside the cloud are concealed to creatures within it. Any creature that enters the area or begins its turn in the area must attempt a Fortitude save with a DC equal to the dragon’s Pyre Breath.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature begins coughing and until the start of its next turn it must succeed at a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) if it attempts any action that requires it to speak, or it loses the action.
**Critical Failure** As failure, plus it must immediately spend its next action coughing. **Inextinguishable Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 90 feet. Flames burn underwater in this aura and cannot be doused by non-magical water. When a creature within this aura attempts a flat check to end [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), it must roll twice and take the worse result. **Inflame Emotions** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon sends out a wave of heat that inflames emotions with 90 feet. Creatures within it must attempt a Will save with a DC equal to the dragon’s Frightful Presence DC. Regardless of the results, they are then immune to Inflame Emotions for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature can spend a reaction to make a melee Strike against a target of its choice.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature immediately makes a melee Strike against a target of the dragon’s choice. The creature cannot use reactions until the start of its next turn.
Cinder Dragon Spellcasters Cinder dragon spellcasters tend to cast the following spells. ### Young Cinder Dragons **Primal Prepared Spells DC** 29, attack +21; **4th** [_fire shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1529), [_mountain resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610), [_wall of fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748); **3rd** [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553); **2nd** [_floating flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1533), [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653); **1st** [_cleanse cuisine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1468), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740); **Cantrips (4th)** [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [_ignition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [_sigil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673) ### Adult Cinder Dragons **Primal Prepared Spells DC** 34, attack +26; As young cinder dragon, plus **6th** [_cursed metamorphosis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), _wall of fire_; **5th** [_blazing bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450), _fireball_, [_toxic cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722); **Cantrips (6th)** _detect magic_, _ignition_, _message_, _read aura_, _sigil_ ### Ancient Cinder Dragon **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 41, attack +33; As adult cinder dragon, plus **9th** [_detonate magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1488), [_falling stars_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1521) (asteroids only); **8th** [_desiccate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1484), _fireball_, [_volcanic eruption_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1746); **7th** [_execute_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519), [_fiery body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1527), _volcanic eruption_; **Cantrips (9th)** _detect magic_, _ignition_, _message_, _read aura_, _sigil_ ### Cinder Archdragon **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 45, attack +37; As ancient cinder dragon, plus **10th** [_cataclysm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1460), [_indestructibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1573); **9th** [_massacre_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1596); **Cantrips (10th)** _detect magic_, _ignition_, _message_, _read aura_, _sigil_
Sheblis and Tyrkalis After terrorizing the orcs of Belkzen for decades, this mated pair of adult cinder dragons reached a tentative peace in return for gold. Amid Tar-Baphon’s return and his sinister interest in their three hatchlings, they have focused their fury on the Whispering Tyrant’s forces, forging not just an armistice but a true alliance to turn back the undead threat. Whether that will hold forever without a resumption of the devastating payments from the orcs, only the dragons know.
Dragon **Related Families** [Dragon, Adamantine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=396), [Dragon, Barrage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=634), [Dragon, Bog](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=635), [Dragon, Brine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=636), [Dragon, Cloud](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=637), [Dragon, Conspirator](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=397), [Dragon, Coral](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=583), [Dragon, Crystal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=638), [Dragon, Delight](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=639), [Dragon, Despair](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=584), [Dragon, Diabolic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=398), [Dragon, Empyreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=399), [Dragon, Executor](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=641), [Dragon, Forest](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=642), [Dragon, Fortune](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=400), [Dragon, Horned](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=401), [Dragon, Magma](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=643), [Dragon, Mirage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=402), [Dragon, Mocking](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=644), [Dragon, Oath](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=645), [Dragon, Omen](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=403), [Dragon, Phase](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=585), [Dragon, Requiem](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=586), [Dragon, Resurrection](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=587), [Dragon, Rime](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=647), [Dragon, Rune](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=588), [Dragon, Sage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=648), [Dragon, Sea](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=649), [Dragon, Sky](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=650), [Dragon, Sovereign](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=652), [Dragon, Stormcrown](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=653), [Dragon, Time](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=655), [Dragon, Umbral](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=656), [Dragon, Underworld](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=657), [Dragon, Vizier](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=658), [Dragon, Vorpal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=659), [Dragon, Wailing](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=660), [Dragon, Whisper](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=589), [Dragon, Wish](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=661), [Dragonet](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=640), [Wyrmwraith](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=662)

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 120 Coral dragons are the primal guardians of the vast living coral reefs found in oceans across Golarion. Even by draconic standards, coral dragons stand out for their arrogance and vanity, boasting inordinate pride in the vibrant hues of their own coral-encrusted scales and the dazzling splendor of the reefs they protect. Coral dragons like to decorate their reefs, which they view as lairs, with objects of beauty, dotted with the occasional bit of valuables like coins. Those who seek the favor of a coral dragon often find the creature easily swayed by flattery, particularly when paired with shiny or brightly colored gifts that complement the scintillating beauty of the dragon and their underwater domain. Members [Adult Coral Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4349) (Creature 12), [Ancient Coral Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4350) (Creature 17), [Coral Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4099) (Creature 21), [Young Coral Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4348) (Creature 8) Group Families [Dragon, Adamantine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=396), [Dragon, Barrage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=634), [Dragon, Bog](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=635), [Dragon, Brine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=636), [Dragon, Cinder](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=582), [Dragon, Cloud](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=637), [Dragon, Conspirator](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=397), [Dragon, Coral](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=583), [Dragon, Crystal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=638), [Dragon, Delight](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=639), [Dragon, Despair](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=584), [Dragon, Diabolic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=398), [Dragon, Empyreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=399), [Dragon, Executor](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=641), [Dragon, Forest](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=642), [Dragon, Fortune](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=400), [Dragon, Horned](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=401), [Dragon, Magma](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=643), [Dragon, Mirage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=402), [Dragon, Mocking](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=644), [Dragon, Oath](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=645), [Dragon, Omen](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=403), [Dragon, Phase](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=585), [Dragon, Requiem](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=586), [Dragon, Resurrection](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=587), [Dragon, Rime](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=647), [Dragon, Rune](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=588), [Dragon, Sage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=648), [Dragon, Sea](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=649), [Dragon, Sky](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=650), [Dragon, Sovereign](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=652), [Dragon, Stormcrown](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=653), [Dragon, Time](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=655), [Dragon, Umbral](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=656), [Dragon, Underworld](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=657), [Dragon, Vizier](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=658), [Dragon, Vorpal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=659), [Dragon, Wailing](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=660), [Dragon, Whisper](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=589), [Dragon, Wish](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=661), [Dragonet](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=640), [Wyrmwraith](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=662)
Alternate Abilities Some coral dragons can raise massive watery tentacles from the seas around them to pummel or grapple their foes. To make a dragon with this ability, replace Draconic Frenzy with the following. **Grasping Waves** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The dragon conjures five watery tentacles from unoccupied water-filled squares of their choice within 30 feet and attempts a single [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the Fortitude DC of every creature adjacent to a tentacle and no more than 10 feet above the water’s surface. On a success, the creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC equal to the DC of the dragon’s Hydraulic Breath). On a critical success, or a success against a creature already grabbed, the target is dragged below the surface and begins to [drown](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439). All tentacles disappear at the end of the dragon’s next turn unless the effect is [Sustained](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) and a new Athletics check is rolled for all existing tentacles. The growths covering a coral dragon’s body can include many different species, some of which produce a potent natural poison. To make a dragon with this ability, replace Biomineralize with the following. **Palytoxin** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) Any creature who takes damage from the dragon’s jaws, claws, or tail attack, or who Strikes and does damage to the dragon with an unarmed attack, takes 1d6 poison damage (2d6 for ancient and archdragons) and must attempt a Fortitude saving throw (DC equal to the DC of the dragon’s Hydraulic Breath).
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature’s [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) condition increases by 1 until the end of its next turn, to a maximum of 3. If the creature is already enfeebled 3, it becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 instead.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature’s enfeebled condition increases by 2 instead of 1. The most powerful coral dragons can call upon the innumerable tiny creatures dwelling within their reefs for aid. To make a dragon with this ability, replace an ancient or archdragon’s Reef Armor with the following. **Primal Tide** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Requirements** The dragon is within the bounds of or no more than 25 feet away from their reef; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The dragon calls tens of thousands of tiny marine creatures from the crevices in their reef, which pour over a 50-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) in a clawing, biting, stinging swarm. Every creature in the area that is fully or partially submerged in water must succeed at a basic Reflex saving throw (DC equal to the DC of the dragon’s Hydraulic Breath) or take piercing damage equal to half that dealt by their Hydraulic Breath and an equivalent amount of poison damage. The dragon can specify any number of creatures within the area to be spared. All summoned creatures disperse at the end of the dragon’s turn.
Coral Dragon Spellcasting Coral dragon spellcasters tend to cast the following spells. ### Young Coral Dragon **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 26, attack +18; **4th** [_hydraulic torrent_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562), [_vapor form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738), **3rd** [_aqueous orb_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1443), [_crashing wave_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1984), [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677); **2nd** [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), [_water breathing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1755), [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756); **1st** [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1438), [_create water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476), [_tailwind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1711); **Cantrips (4th)** [detect magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2624), [_know the way_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [_spout_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2031), [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) ### Adult Coral Dragon **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 32, attack +24; As young coral dragon, plus **6th** [_chameleon coat_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1973), [_petrify_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1628); **5th** [_control water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473), _crashing wave_, [_mariner’s curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1593); **4th** [_mirage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604); **Cantrips (6th)** _detect magic_, _know the way_, _prestidigitation_, _spout_, _stabilize_; **Rituals** DC 32; [_commune_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=114) (nature only) ### Ancient Coral Dragon **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 38, attack +30; As adult coral dragon, plus **8th** _crashing wave_, [_desiccate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1484), [_moment of renewal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1607); **7th** _hydraulic torrent_, [_regenerate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1648), [_unfettered pack_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1733); **6th** _slow_; **Cantrips (8th)** _detect magic_, _know the way_, _prestidigitation_, _spout_, _stabilize_; **Rituals** DC 38; _commune_ (nature only), [control weather](/Rituals.aspx?ID=116) ### Coral Archdragon **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 44, attack +36; As ancient coral dragon plus **10th** [_nature incarnate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1612); **9th** [_implosion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1572), [_wrathful storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1759); **Cantrips (10th)** _detect magic_, _know the way_, _prestidigitation_, _spout_, _stabilize_; **Rituals** DC 44; _commune (Nature only)_, _control weather_
Kamakanaka The ancient coral dragon Kamakanaka proudly reigns as the self-styled master of the Moluune Parapet, a colossal reef that sprawls for miles beneath the lavaheated waters surrounding a massive volcanic island in the western Shackles. Travelers braving the treacherous waters often make offerings to Kamakanaka by casting coins, flowers, and jewelry into the sea in hopes of safe passage via direct guidance from the dragon.
Dragon **Related Families** [Dragon, Adamantine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=396), [Dragon, Barrage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=634), [Dragon, Bog](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=635), [Dragon, Brine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=636), [Dragon, Cinder](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=582), [Dragon, Cloud](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=637), [Dragon, Conspirator](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=397), [Dragon, Crystal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=638), [Dragon, Delight](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=639), [Dragon, Despair](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=584), [Dragon, Diabolic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=398), [Dragon, Empyreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=399), [Dragon, Executor](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=641), [Dragon, Forest](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=642), [Dragon, Fortune](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=400), [Dragon, Horned](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=401), [Dragon, Magma](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=643), [Dragon, Mirage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=402), [Dragon, Mocking](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=644), [Dragon, Oath](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=645), [Dragon, Omen](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=403), [Dragon, Phase](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=585), [Dragon, Requiem](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=586), [Dragon, Resurrection](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=587), [Dragon, Rime](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=647), [Dragon, Rune](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=588), [Dragon, Sage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=648), [Dragon, Sea](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=649), [Dragon, Sky](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=650), [Dragon, Sovereign](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=652), [Dragon, Stormcrown](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=653), [Dragon, Time](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=655), [Dragon, Umbral](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=656), [Dragon, Underworld](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=657), [Dragon, Vizier](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=658), [Dragon, Vorpal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=659), [Dragon, Wailing](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=660), [Dragon, Whisper](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=589), [Dragon, Wish](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=661), [Dragonet](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=640), [Wyrmwraith](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=662)

<title level="1" right="Creature Family">[Dragon, Despair](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=584)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 122

Fear is one of the most powerful emotions, and despair dragons are masters of channeling those feelings of terror and hopelessness for their own benefit. As with other occult dragons, despair dragons are driven by an innate compulsion, in their case, the desire to strike terror in others. Despair dragons tend to settle near settlements, so they have a source of fear to draw from. The most common items in despair dragon hoards are scrolls, tomes, and relics that serve as research material. Through these, despair dragons learn of local folklore, urban legends, and more to better haunt their targets.

<title level="2">Members</title>
[Adult Despair Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4352) (Creature 13), [Ancient Despair Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4353) (Creature 18), [Despair Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4100) (Creature 22), [Young Despair Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4351) (Creature 9)

[Dragonet](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=640), [Wyrmwraith](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=662)

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons//">Alternate Abilities</title>

Despair dragons can develop different ways of using fear to their advantage. You can apply the following adjustments to a despair dragon of any age.

Despair dragons who specialize in whipping a populace into hysteria using urban legends and regional fears can shape this terror into a semi-physical form. To make a dragon with this ability, replace Unbidden Thoughts with the following.

**Manifest Fear** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The despair dragon creates an illusory image of a creature referenced in local superstition. This functions as illusory creature, heightened to a rank equal to half the dragon’s level. The damage die of the illusory creature’s Strikes increases to d6s against creatures with the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition. Some despair dragons can implant false memories in the minds of others. To make a dragon with this ability, replace Draconic Momentum with the following. 

**Cursed Memories** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 90 feet. Creatures near a despair dragon can't trust their own minds. When any creature in the aura attempts a skill check to Recall Knowledge, they must roll twice and take the lower result.

The best place to sow fear is from the shadows. To make a dragon with this ability, replace Draconic Frenzy with the following.

**Lights Out** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The despair dragon belches an impenetrable darkness that encompasses a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Light does not enter the area and any non-magical light sources, such as a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760) or [lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2730), do not emanate any light while inside the area, even if their light radius would extend beyond the darkness. This also suppresses magical light of a spell rank equal to half the dragon's level or lower. Light can't pass through, so all creatures in the area except for the dragon can't see outside.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Despair Dragon Spellcasting</title>

Despair dragon spellcasters tend to cast the following spells. 

### Young Despair Dragon
**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 28, attack +20; **4th** [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615); **3rd** [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602), [_noise blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677); **2nd** [_paranoia_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1623), [_stupefy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691), [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740); **1st** [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [_ill omen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675); **Cantrips (4th)** [_detect scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1487); **Cantrips (6th)** [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [_figment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [_haunting hymn_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1999), [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [_telekinetic projectile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) 

### Adult Despair Dragon
**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 33, attack +25; As young despair dragon, plus **6th** [_mislead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1605), [_phantasmal calamity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630); **5th** [_strange geometry_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2032), [_synaptic pulse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710), [_wave of despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757); **4th** _detect scrying_; **Cantrips (6th)** _detect magic_, _figment_, _haunting hymn_, _message_, _telekinetic projectile_

### Ancient Despair Dragon
**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 40, attack +32; As adult despair dragon, plus **8th** [_canticle of everlasting grief_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1459), [_hidden mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556), [_pinpoint_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633); **7th** [_mask of terror_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1595), [_visions of danger_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2040), wave of despair; **6th** [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662); **Cantrips (8th)** _detect magic_, _figment_, _haunting hymn_, _message_, _telekinetic projectile_o	

### Despair Archdragon
**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 45, attack +37; As ancient despair dragon plus **10th** [_fabricated truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1520); **9th** [_overwhelming presence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1621), [_phantasmagoria_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1629), [_wails of the damned_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1747); **Cantrips (10th)** _detect magic_, _figment_, _haunting hymn_, _message_, _telekinetic projectile_
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Thyndred</title>

Not many bother to ask the name of the unassuming elven librarian in the archives of Pangolais, but only a precious few know her true identity. When she descends into the dampness of her underground lair, she resumes the form of the despair dragon Thyndred. She is careful to avoid notice while above the surface, but she has found that a well-placed scroll—or perhaps a timely nightmare—can have quite the influence on the alreadydraconian workings of the Umbral Court. This gradual escalation of distress among members is particularly nourishing for Thyndred.
</aside>

<title level="2" right="Creature Family Group">Dragon</title>

<title level="1" right="Creature Family">[Dragon, Phase](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=585)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 124

No place can contain a phase dragon or even hold their interest for long; their innate arcane connection ties them to teleportation and repositioning magic. Explorers and scholars, phase dragons move about at will, discovering new locales and the arcane secrets of teleportation. They frequently establish multiple lairs in far-flung places they repeatedly visit. Beyond the typical wealth found in lairs, phase dragons tend to keep items of sentimental value from their travels, such as a particularly rare flower from the region or a piece by a local artist.

<title level="2">Members</title>
[Adult Phase Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4355) (Creature 13), [Ancient Phase Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4356) (Creature 18), [Phase Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4106) (Creature 22), [Young Phase Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4354) (Creature 9)

<title level="2">Group Families</title>
[Dragon, Adamantine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=396), [Dragon, Barrage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=634), [Dragon, Bog](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=635), [Dragon, Brine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=636), <aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons//">Alternate Abilities</title>

Some phase dragons’ mastery of teleportation manifests in different ways. You can apply the following adjustments to a phase dragon of any age. 

Some phase dragons can appear to be in two places at once. To make a dragon with such an ability, remove flicker from their innate spells and replace Shoo! with either of the following. 

**Afterimage Jumps** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The phase dragon teleports in a quick series of jumps around their own location, creating three illusory images of themself that are hard to distinguish from the real thing. Any foe targeting the phase dragon with an attack must first succeed at a DC 15 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a failure, the phase dragon isn’t affected and one of the illusory duplicates is shattered; the DC of the next flat check for Afterimage Jumps is reduced by 5. Any remaining images disappear at the start of the dragon’s next turn. 

Second Glance <actions string="Single Action" /> ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) If the phase dragon’s next action is to use an ability or spell with the [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) trait, they become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) when they arrive at their destination and leave an illusory duplicate at their initial location. The invisibility ends and the illusory duplicate disappears if the dragon takes a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251); if a creature [interacts](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) with the false image, including attempting to Strike it; or at the start of the dragon’s next turn, whichever comes first. 

Rarely, a phase dragon can tune their forms to be slightly out of phase with reality. To make a dragon with such an ability, remove flicker from their innate spells and replace Portal Strike with the following. 

Dematerialize <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The phase dragon becomes incorporeal, gaining the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) trait, resistance to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), and [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) equal to half their level (double the resistance against non-magical damage), and immunity to precision damage. If the dragon attempts a Strike against a creature with any circumstance bonuses to AC (such as from a [shield](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12) or [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372)), reduce that bonus by 2. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until the phase dragon [Dismisses](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) it.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Phase Dragon Spellcasters</title>

Phase dragon spellcasters tend to cast the following spells.

### Young Phase Dragon
**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 28, attack +20; **4th** [_liminal doorway_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1587), [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732); **3rd** [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [_safe passage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659); **2nd** [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [_humanoid form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1560); **1st** [_ant haul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1442), [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [_tailwind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1711); **Cantrips (4th)** [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [_figment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [_telekinetic projectile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)

### Adult Phase Dragon
**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 33, attack +25; As young phase dragon, plus **6th** [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662), [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1749); **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), _dispel magic_, [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665); **4th** [_mirage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604); **Cantrips (6th)** _detect magic_, _figment_, _message_, _read aura_, _telekinetic projectile_

### Ancient Phase Dragon
**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 40, attack +32; As adult phase dragon, plus **8th** _dispel magic_, [_pinpoint_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633), [_teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720); **7th** [_contingency_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1472), [_energy aegis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1512), [_retrocognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652); **6th** [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492); **Cantrips (8th)** _detect magic_, _figment_, _message_, _read aura_, _telekinetic projectile_o	

### Phase Archdragon
**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 45, attack +37; As ancient phase dragon  plus **10th** [_freeze time_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1538), [_gate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1540); **9th** [_detonate magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1488), [_implosion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1572), [_metamorphosis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1599); **Cantrips (10th)** _detect magic_, _figment_, _message_, _read aura_, _telekinetic projectile_
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Zehanlian</title>

Early in her life in Kyonin, the young phase dragon Zehanlian became captivated by _aiudaras_. Now that they’re her primary interest, she relentlessly delves into the secrets of these elf gates. A cadre of assembled adventurers and staff aid her, establishing safe camps at each new destination an elf gate leads them to while she and a band of mostly elven scholars focus on her studies. Zehanlian’s travels recently brought her to the planet Castrovel, where she established a lair with easy access to the magical transit hub Telasia.
</aside>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 126 Requiem dragons are stewards of the River of Souls and the process through which souls reach their final destination in the afterlife. The safe journey of a soul is of utmost importance to a requiem dragon, and some will follow individual souls from their first entry into the river through to their judgment in the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) and eventually to their ultimate resting place. Most requiem dragons tie themselves to specific planes and shepherd any souls bound to that plane, leading to dragons linked to places like [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41) or [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), though these dragons never swear true allegiance to these planes. Requiem dragons fill their lairs along the River of Souls—patchworks of somber architecture rescued from other planes—with invaluable records and mementos of great accomplishments. Members [Adult Requiem Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4358) (Creature 15), [Ancient Requiem Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4359) (Creature 20), [Requiem Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4107) (Creature 24), [Young Requiem Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4357) (Creature 11) Group Families [Dragon, Adamantine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=396), [Dragon, Barrage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=634), [Dragon, Alternate Abilities A requiem dragon’s control over souls manifests in various ways. You can apply the following adjustments to a requiem dragon of any age. Some requiem dragons are spiteful enough to try to bring a living creature along with them when they die. To create a dragon with this ability, replace Withhold Death with the following. **Die Together** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Trigger** The dragon dies; **Effect** The dragon tethers their soul to the soul of a living creature within 60 feet, pulling it along into death. The target must attempt a Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes void damage equal to half the damage dealt by the dragon’s Dooming Breath.
**Failure** The target takes void damage equal to the damage dealt by the dragon’s Dooming Breath.
**Critical Failure** The target drops to 0 Hit Points and becomes [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) 1, or increases its dying condition by 1 if it’s already dying. While the target is dying, the dragon remains at 1 Hit Point and dies instantly if the target loses the dying condition. Requiem dragons who specialize in fighting against and alongside armies can let out a mighty roar that frightens their enemies and bolsters their allies. To create a dragon with this ability, replace Draconic Frenzy with the following. **Soul Roar** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The dragon lets out a mighty roar that rouses their allies and rattles the souls of their enemies. Each ally in a 120-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gains [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to the dragon’s level that last for 1 minute, while each enemy within that area must attempt a Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is immune to Soul Roar for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2 and [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1. If the target is already doomed, the doomed value increases by 1 (to a maximum of doomed 4).
**Critical Failure** The creature gains the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) condition for 1 round, is frightened 4, and is doomed 1. If the target is already doomed, the doomed value increases by 1 (to a maximum of doomed 4).
Requiem Dragon Spellcasters Requiem dragon spellcasters tend to cast the following spells. ### Young Requiem Dragon **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 30, attack +22; **4th** [_ghostly tragedy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1995), [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712), [_vital beacon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1743); **3rd** [_protection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1641), [_ring of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656), [_safe passage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659); **2nd** [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [_share life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1669), [_thoughtful gift_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2037); **1st** _protection_, [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660), [_spirit link_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1686); **Cantrips (4th)** [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [_haunting hymn_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1999), [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [_sigil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673), [stabilize](/Rules.aspx?ID=1381) ### Adult Requiem Dragon **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 36, attack +28; As young requiem dragon, plus **6th** [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1526), [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645), _vital beacon_; **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_breath of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1456), [_invoke spirits_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578); **Cantrips (6th)** _guidance_, _haunting hymn_, _message_, _sigil_, _stabilize_ ### Ancient Requiem Dragon Divine Prepared Spells DC 42, attack +34; As adult requiem dragon, plus **9th** [_foresight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537), _raise dead_, [_seize soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664); **8th** _field of life_, _safe passage_, [_spirit song_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2029); **7th** [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), [_planar seal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1635), [_regenerate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1648); **Cantrips (9th)** _guidance_, _haunting hymn_ , _message_, _sigil_, _stabilize_o ### Requiem Archdragon **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 48, attack +40; As ancient requiem dragon plus **10th** [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645), [_revival_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1654); **9th** [_breath of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1456); **Cantrips (10th)** _guidance_, _haunting hymn_, _message_, _sigil_, _stabilize_
Roum The requiem dragon Roum, a cleric of [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=333), tasked herself with escorting the souls of inventors down the River of Souls. She records their final ideas in a vast library she calls the Hall of Lost Invention. She keeps this lair secreted away in a cavern in the Brazen Peaks. From here, she can spy on the works of local inventors. Roum will occasionally petition for the resurrection of unfortunate inventors who lost their lives to their own risky but promising creations—magical or mundane—if their idea intrigues her.

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 128 Resurrection dragons teeter between life and death. They have a mastery of vital energies, allowing them to restore life to the dead, and a mastery of void energies, to bestow death on others. They make use of their abilities to play with the lives of mortals, calling on spirits to aid them or reviving creatures they find important or interesting. The lair of a resurrection dragon is generally a barren place. While they still hoard wealth like other dragons, they do little to decorate their lairs and treasures are generally kept in dark niches, as if the dragon has little care for their possessions. Resurrection dragons tend to take tokens from those they resurrect or plan to resurrect, however, and these are kept particularly safe. Members [Adult Resurrection Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4361) (Creature 12), [Ancient Resurrection Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4362) (Creature 17), [Resurrection Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4108) Alternate Abilities Some resurrection dragons can control life and death in different ways. You can apply the following adjustments to a resurrection dragon of any age. As a resurrection dragon heals themself, they can simultaneously weaken the living around them. To create a dragon with this ability, replace Draconic Momentum with the following. **Shred Souls** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Trigger** The resurrection dragon ends their turn; **Requirements** The dragon’s Soul Siphoning Breath recharged this turn; **Effect** Excess void energy spills out from the dragon in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Each living creature in the area must succeed at a Fortitude save with a DC equal to the dragon’s Soul Siphoning Breath or gain [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) to void equal to the dragon’s resistance to spirit for 1 round (or 1 minute on a critical failure). Resurrection dragons who focus their power into slaying as much of the living as possible can wreath their claws in void energy. To create a dragon with this ability, replace Draconic Frenzy with the following. **Harmful Claw** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The resurrection dragon channels a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spell into their claw. They Cast a 1-action version of [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), but the effects of the spell don’t occur immediately. The dragon then makes a claw Strike. This counts as two attacks for the dragon’s multiple attack penalty. The attack is imbued with the spell’s effects according to the results of the Strike.
**Critical Success** The Strike deals double damage as normal, and the target must attempt a basic Fortitude save against the spell’s damage, but treats its result as one degree of success worse.
**Success** The Strike deals damage as normal, and the target attempts a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save against the spell’s damage.
**Failure** The Strike deals no damage, but the target must attempt a basic Fortitude save against the spell’s damage.
**Critical Failure** The Strike deals no damage, and the target is unaffected by the spell. More necromancy-focused resurrection dragons enjoy creating permanent [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) minions. To create a dragon with this ability, give the dragon the [_create undead_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=117) ritual and replace Risen Commander with the following. **Deathless Servant** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) When a resurrection dragon performs the [_create undead_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=117) ritual, they can create any common undead and don’t require secondary casters. If the dragon creates a [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) undead creature whose level is at least 2 lower than themself, it automatically becomes the dragon’s minion—a deathless servant. This minion can’t be destroyed unless the dragon is slain; if reduced to 0 Hit Points, such a minion returns to full Hit Points 1d6 days later. A resurrection dragon can have only one deathless servant at a time.
Resurrection Dragon Spellcasting Resurrection dragon spellcasters tend to cast the following spells. ### Young Resurrection Dragon **Divine Innate Spells** DC 26, attack +18; **3rd** [_final sacrifice_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1992), [_sudden blight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2033), [_vampiric feast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736); **2nd** [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [_share life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1669), [_spirit sense_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2028); **1st** [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [_infuse vitality_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1574), [_grim tendrils_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548); **Cantrips (3rd)** [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [_haunting hymn_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1999), [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) ### Adult Resurrection Dragon **Divine Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24; As young resurrection dragon, plus **5th** [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), _heal_, [_invoke spirits_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578); **4th** [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [_summon undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706), [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712); **Cantrips (5th)** _detect magic_, _guidance_, _haunting hymn_, _read aura_, _stabilize_ ### Ancient Resurrection Dragon **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +30; As adult resurrection dragon, plus **8th** [_moment of renewal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1607), [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645), _summon undead_; **7th** [_execute_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519), _harm_, [_regenerate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1648); **6th** [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1526), _raise dead_, _summon undead_; **Cantrips (8th)** _detect magic_, _guidance_, _haunting hymn_, _read aura_, _stabilize_ ### Resurrection Archdragon **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 46, attack +38; As ancient resurrection dragon plus **10th** [_revival_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1654); **9th** [_massacre_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1596), [_wails of the damned_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1747); **Cantrips (10th)** _detect magic_, _guidance_, _haunting hymn_, _read aura_, _stabilize_
Revirescena and Esurek Revirescena and Esurek are a rare mother-son pair of resurrection dragons located on the western coast of the Lake of Mists and Veils who maintain a close bond out of both affection and necessity. Born sickly, Esurek was unable to properly defend himself and eventually met his demise at the hand of a party of adventurers seeking wealth and glory. One of the adventurer’s weapons disrupted Esurek’s soul, forcing Revirescena to send her own soul to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) to pull her son’s soul back to his body, chaining their souls together in the process. Revirescena is unable to fully restore Esurek’s disrupted soul. She has become a doting, overprotective parent, seeking a way to return her son’s soul to normal but unable to leave her son’s side long enough to seek help.

<title level="1" right="Creature Family">[Dragon, Rune](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=588)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 131

Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes. Most rune dragons keep a variety of runes etched on their scales, which their bodies can magically empower. They typically don't cast spells themselves, instead relying on their ability to create runes on the fly to produce desired effects. A rune dragon often refers to their hoard as their library, as it's full of important historical texts, plays, speeches, textbooks, and spellbooks written by people of different cultures in a variety of languages. While they can often speak a language with the aid of magic, they much prefer to learn languages in the context of their speakers so they can grasp the nuances hidden within the syntax and the magic within their symbols.

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons//">Alternate Abilities</title>

Many rune dragons find alternative ways to utilize their mastery of runes. Any one of the following abilities can be mastered by rune dragons. Remove Entangling Rune and replace it with one of the following.

### Adult Rune Dragon:
 **Runic Jail** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The rune dragon conjures a prison that promises pain to those within and those who try to escape. The dragon creates a runic jail in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) up to 60 feet away. The area is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and creatures outside the jail become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to creatures within it. If a creature uses a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action to leave the runic jail, they gain a detonating rune. The runic jail lasts for 1 round, but a creature adjacent to the jail, but not within it, can use an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to remove the rune early.

**Catalyzing Rune** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The dragon creates a highly reactive rune in a 30-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) up to 60 feet away. Whenever a creature takes damage from a detonating rune while within the area, all other creatures in the area take the damage of the detonating rune ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save DC 34). The Catalyzing Rune lasts for 1 minute but can be removed if creatures adjacent to the rune spend two [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions.

### Ancient Rune Dragon:
 **Runic Jail** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) As adult rune dragon, but the area is a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385).

**Catalyzing Rune** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) As adult rune dragon, but the area is a 40-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) and the DC is 41.

### Rune Archdragon:
 **Runic Jail** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) As adult rune dragon, but the area is a 30-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385).

**Catalyzing Rune** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) As adult rune dragon, but the area is a 60-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) and the DC is 46.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Runedragon Spellcasters</title>

If a spell the rune dragon casts would deal energy damage, it instead deals the damage type associated with its Shifting Runes. Additionally, rune dragon spellcasters know the [rune trap](/Rituals.aspx?ID=124) ritual and can fulfill the role of the secondary caster themselves. Rune dragon spellcasters tend to focus on specific types of magic such as ice magic or summoning. The following represents a rune dragon with a broader array of spells.

### Young Rune Dragon
**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 29, attack +21; **4th** [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723), [_wall of fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748); **3rd** [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [_gravity well_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1997), [_veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739); **2nd** [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [_embed message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1511), _translate_; **1st** [_alarm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1439), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550); **Cantrips (4th)** [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [_ignition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [_sigil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673), [_telekinetic hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)

### Adult Rune Dragon
**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 34, attack +26; As young rune dragon, plus **6th** [_chain lightning_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462), [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662), [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1749); **5th** [_howling blizzard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559), [_imaginary lockbox_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2002), [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728); **Cantrips (6th)** _detect magic_, _ignition_, _message_, _sigil_, _telekinetic hand_

### Ancient Rune Dragon
**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 41, attack +33; As adult rune dragon, plus **9th** [_detonate magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1488), [_falling stars_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1521), [_foresight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537); **8th** [_hidden mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556), [_quandary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644), [_unrelenting observation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1734); **7th** _fireball_, [_planar seal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1635), [_spell riposte_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2027); **Cantrips (9th)** _detect magic_, _ignition_, _message_, _sigil_, _telekinetic hand_o	

###  Rune Archdragon
**Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 46, attack +38; As ancient rune dragon plus **10th** [_cataclysm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1460); **9th** [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [_implosion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1572); **Cantrips (10th)** _detect magic_, _ignition_, _message_, _sigil_, _telekinetic hand_
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Trohalix</title>

The rune dragon Trohalix keeps a lair hidden in the deserts of Thuvia. She ensures that rumors of her vastly supplied library are well known, but the exact location is not. Those who seek her knowledge can access the library for a time, depending on their donation, and anyone foolish enough to steal from her rarely lives to tell the tale.
</aside>

<title level="2" right="Creature Family Group">Dragon</title>

**Related Families**

<title level="1" right="Creature Family">[Dragon, Whisper](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=589)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 133

Whisper dragons are keen collectors of rumors and secrets who spend centuries honing their information networks and relationships. They generally do so not out of an intent to hold the information over others or to use for their own machinations, but simply because the process of learning and gathering information is fulfilling. Their hoards are sparse compared to those of other dragons, as they hold their true treasures—secrets—in their minds.

<title level="2">Members</title>
[Adult Whisper Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4367) (Creature 11), [Ancient Whisper Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4368) (Creature 16), [Whisper Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4110) (Creature 20), [Young Whisper Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4366) (Creature 7)

<title level="2">Group Families</title>
[Dragon, Adamantine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=396), [Dragon, Barrage](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=634), [Dragon, Bog](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=635), [Dragon, Brine](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=636), [Dragonet](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=640), [Wyrmwraith](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=662)

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons//">Alternate Abilities</title>

Whisper dragons can develop different skills and abilities than are typical. You can apply the following adjustments to whisper dragons of any age.

Some whisper dragons bind an informant’s secrets into contracts, obtaining their information in exchange for granting them a boon. To make a dragon with this ability, replace Steal Knowledge with the following. 

**Draft Contract** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The dragon produces a contract for a single living mortal. This contract can grant a single boon or ability, the extent of which is dependent on the power of the dragon, in exchange for one piece of knowledge the mortal possesses. The mortal must willingly sign their true name to the contract, as well as the knowledge they wish to surrender. Upon signing, the mortal forgets the bartered information, which cannot be restored by any means short of a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual; if they restore this knowledge, they lose the boon granted by the contract.

A whisper dragon can develop strong bonds with their informants that allow them to view the world through their informants’ senses. To make a dragon with this ability, replace Information Network with the following ability.

**Share Sight** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689)) The dragon states the name of an allied informant within 100 miles, giving them a mental alert. The informant can choose whether to accept the contact. If they consent, the dragon projects their senses into the informant’s, allowing them to perceive through their informant. When they do, they lose all sensory information from their own body. The dragon can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this effect for up to 1 hour and [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) it as a free action. A whisper dragon might take on a humanoid guise to gather information without attracting notice. To make a dragon with this ability, give them the following ability.

**Change Shape** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The dragon takes on the appearance of a Small or Medium humanoid. Their humanoid shape has a specific, persistent appearance. In humanoid shape, the dragon loses their jaws, claws, and tail Strikes, but retains their attack bonuses with any weapons they wield. Their Speed is reduced to 25 feet.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Naizraa</title>

The whisper dragon Naizraa resides in a sumptuous palace high on the bluffs of northern Qadira’s Saray Coast. She keeps a close eye on the intrigues of Qadira’s nobility and sometimes offers her patronage to talented individuals seeking information or influence, in exchange for a secret of their own.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Whisper Dragon Spellcasting</title>

Whisper dragon spellcasters tend to cast the following spells.

### Young Whisper Dragon
**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 25, attack +17; **3rd** [_hypercognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1563), [_ring of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656); **2nd** [_clear mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [_humanoid form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1560), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663); **1st** [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [_disguise magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1491), [_mindlink_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603); **Cantrips (3rd)** [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535), [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [_telekinetic hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)

### Adult Whisper Dragon
**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 30, attack +22; As young whisper dragon, plus **5th** [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665), [_synaptic pulse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710); **4th** [_confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [_detect scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1487), [_rewrite memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655); **3rd** [_dream message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503); **Cantrips (5th)** _daze_, _detect magic_, _forbidding ward_, _message_, _telekinetic hand_

### Ancient Whisper Dragon
**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 37, attack +29; As adult whisper dragon, plus **7th** [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), [_project image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1640), [_retrocognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652); **6th** [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662), [_teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720), [_zealous conviction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1760); **5th** [_scouting eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661); **Cantrips (7th)** _daze_, _detect magic_, _forbidding ward_, _message_, _telekinetic hand_o	

###  Whisper Archdragon
**Occult Prepared Spells** DC 42, attack +34; As ancient whisper dragon plus **9th** [_overwhelming presence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1621), [_telepathic demand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2036); **8th** [_disappearance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1490), [_hidden mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556), [_unrelenting observation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1734); **Cantrips (9th)** _daze_, _detect magic_, _forbidding ward_, _message_, _telekinetic hand_

<title level="1" right="Creature Family">[Dragonblood](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=590)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 136

When a dragon holds influence in a community, either by exerting their might or blending in and living among them, a child might be born that exhibits physical or mental aspects of that dragon. These offspring are known as dragonbloods and often have draconic features belying their heritage. Dragonbloods can be found among all ancestries, though many dragonbloods are either [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6) or have some kind of extant dragon connection, such as [kobolds](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=18) who have a close association with a dragon.

 A dragonblood typically possesses some physical feature that gives away their draconic nature. These can be subtle features such as elongated fingernails that resemble claws or unique eye coloration. For many dragonbloods, these features are more overt, taking the form of horns, draconic tails, wings, or patches of scales. In some cases, a dragonblood resembles a bipedal dragon outright, often leading to confusing them with other reptilian ancestries such as kobolds and lizardfolk. For dragonbloods who have no obvious features, there are still the occasional hints of their draconic influence, typically manifesting in times of overwhelming emotions, if only for a moment.

 Dragonbloods can trace their connection to a specific type of dragon, commonly referred to as a draconic exemplar. The physical features and abilities of a dragonblood always match that of their draconic exemplar. A dragonblood whose exemplar is a cinder dragon, for example, will manifest crimson scales or the ability to breathe fire. A dragon's opinion of a related dragonblood can vary. Most dragons tend to look at dragonbloods with indifference. Benevolent and social dragons often find a kinship in learning of a related dragonblood. Some dragons see dragonbloods as beneath them, however. As a dragonblood isn't a “true” dragon, the draconic exemplar cares little for them.

<title level="2">Members</title>
[Dragonblood Occultist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4369) (Creature 3)
name: Dragonblood

<title level="1" right="Creature Family">[Dragonfly](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=591)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 138

Dragonflies hunt with a combination of agile power and deadly speed. In early life stages, these insects are entirely aquatic predators, but they take to the air once they've molted. Most live around bodies of water suitable for spawning, but giant dragonflies have been known to fly many miles while on the hunt. Though their gossamer wings and colorful bodies are beautiful at first glance, an unwary adventurer lured in by the display runs a very real risk of becoming lunch.

<title level="2">Members</title>
[Giant Dragonfly](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4372) (Creature 4), [Giant Dragonfly Nymph](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4371) (Creature 3)

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Dragonfly Species</title>

While dragonflies come in many colors, the differences between species sometimes extend beyond aesthetic variation. Giant blue dragonflies are larger, toxic versions of the insects with a numbing bite that boggards use to craft poisons. Cave dragonflies are slower but stronger variants that dwell in large cavern systems. The legendary storm dragonflies are truly immense creatures that can use their powerful wings to stun foes.
</aside>
name: Dragonfly

<title level="1" right="Creature Family">[Elemental, Wisp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=592)</title>

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 152

Wisps are tiny elemental beings that emerged during the creation of the Elemental Planes. The first wisps roamed the Inner Sphere in shifting symphonies that traded members with their every meeting. These symphonies of free wisps created music out of their combined resonances, but when the wicked Elemental Lords realized the value of the wisps' resonance, they captured whole symphonies for use as servants.

 Wisps attune to each other and to those they surround themselves with. This attunement makes them naturally supportive allies. Free wisps still roam the Inner Sphere and [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), but they're usually shy and hide themselves from strangers. However, they happily offer their service to those who show them kindness. They especially find themselves drawn to spellcasters who practice elemental magic; these wisps usually watch from afar but sometimes approach those who seem safe to become familiars or comrades.

 Free wisps who find one another become close almost immediately. They get especially excited when they find wisps of elements other than their own, showing none of the animosity sometimes exhibited between elementals of different planes. Instead, they join in an excited dance, emitting resonances that faintly echo the symphonies of ancient days.

<title level="2">Members</title>
[Air Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4394) (Creature 0), [Earth Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4395) (Creature 0), [Fire Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4396) (Creature 0), [Water Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4397) (Creature 0)

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Wisp Symphonies</title>

Dozens or even hundreds of wisps of all types can gather in a wisp symphony, usually with one or more wisp conductors. These groups are rare now that the majority of their kind exists in servitude to the [Elemental Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=8), but a few free symphonies still roam the Elemental Planes, and legends tell of the transcendent songs they produce together. These groups of wisps use the rules for a swarm and can’t Accord Essence, but collectively they can produce damaging area effects or cast certain spells.
</aside>
name: Elemental, Wisp
